search_queries_used: site:rcem.ac.uk invasive procedures emergency department October 2023 consent site verification documentation audit
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic consent, success, complication or post-procedure conclusion
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A generic note cannot safely describe technique for every procedure.
Consent, performance and outcome must be explicitly clinician-documented.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children undergoing an invasive or high-risk ED procedure; procedure-specific contraindications and competencies remain separate.
setting_applicability: Emergency procedures performed by qualified staff under local policy.
UAE_applicability: Documentation concepts transfer, but UAE credentialing, consent, capacity, site verification, infection control, sedation and monitoring policies govern.
recency_verification: The official RCEM October 2023 update was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The October 2023 update is current in the RCEM catalogue.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact RCEM procedure-safety section supports documenting patient concerns, clinician risk context, procedure-related assessment, severity, appearance, clinician plan, specialist discussion, completed consent and post-procedure handover only when applicable; it does not select a procedure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of consent, site verification, proposed procedure and approach, team communication, checklist use and monitoring requirements.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact audit section supports recording a completed procedure report and measured or reviewed data only when performed; it does not imply successful completion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting consent or the reason consent was impossible, site verification and sedation-proforma use when applicable.

unresolved_source_gaps: Procedure, indication, operator, assistants, site, laterality, consent basis, checklist, preparation, anaesthesia, equipment, technique, findings, specimen, complications, monitoring and aftercare remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
